tool.name: msbar
tool.summary: Apply explicit point mutations to sequence records

sections.title: Overview
sections.content: `msbar` applies one or more explicit point substitutions to every sequence record in one local input. The Epithema v1 surface is bounded to deterministic substitution-only editing using `position:residue` syntax.

sections.title: Inputs
sections.content: The current interface accepts one local sequence input followed by one or more mutation specifications written as `position:residue`. Positions are 1-based, must be unique within a single invocation, and are applied to every input record.

sections.title: Outputs
sections.content: The tool emits mutated FASTA records in input order. CLI summaries report the source input, mutation count, mutation syntax, and FASTA output format.

sections.title: Current Status
sections.content: This method is implemented and exposed through `epithema msbar`. Validation currently covers deterministic substitution behavior against committed FASTA fixtures in the Rust tool and service layers.

sections.title: Caveats
sections.content: The v1 implementation supports substitutions only. It does not support insertions, deletions, ambiguous edit grammars, or annotation-coordinate remapping, and it drops feature annotations after mutation.

artifacts.label: msbar FASTA fixture
artifacts.description: Repository-managed FASTA input fixture used for deterministic point-mutation validation.

examples.title: Apply two point mutations to one input record
examples.description: Runs `msbar` against the committed FASTA fixture and applies mutations `2:T` and `4:A`.

expected_outputs.label: Mutated sequence record
expected_outputs.description: The output FASTA record contains residues `ATGA`, and the shared result summary reports that two mutations were applied using `position:residue` syntax.
